flashcards.front: What is a package in Java?
flashcards.back: A namespace that groups related classes and interfaces into a logical group.

flashcards.front: What are the four access modifiers in Java?
What is the purpose of the public access modifier in Java?
flashcards.back: To make a member accessible from any class in any package.
The concept of bundling data and methods while restricting direct access to some components.

flashcards.front: How does the protected access modifier differ from the default access modifier?
flashcards.back: The protected access modifier makes a member accessible within the same package and subclasses in different packages, while the default access modifier makes a member accessible only within the same package.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of import statements in Java?
flashcards.back: To make classes and interfaces from other packages available in a class.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a single class import and a wildcard import?
flashcards.back: A single class import imports a specific class, while a wildcard import imports all classes in a package.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the private access modifier in Java?
flashcards.back: To make a member accessible only within the same class.
